analysis.summary: The song is a raw and emotional declaration of self-respect and emotional boundaries in a toxic relationship, expressing the pain of loving someone who repeatedly causes harm, while refusing to be the source of their pain.

sections.sectionName: Post-Chorus - Male Ghost Vocal Behind Her
Verse 2 - Male Ghost Vocal, Fragile Melodic Rap

keywords.imageryPrompt: A flickering flame in the dark, defiant and unyielding, casting long shadows on a cold, empty room.
A person slumped on a rain-soaked bench, eyes closed, the sound of distant thunder echoing in the silence.
A deep purple mark on skin, glowing faintly under the dim light of a flickering bulb.
A faint, translucent figure standing in a hallway, watching silently as the lights flicker and die.
A silver blade glinting under moonlight, resting on a table with a red cloth, the air thick with tension.
A single beam of golden light cutting through a stormy night, illuminating a path through the darkness.
A person clutching their chest, tears streaming down their face, as a storm rages outside.
A couple standing in a rainstorm, arms wrapped around each other, eyes closed, whispering promises to the wind.
A glass cup perched on the edge of a table, trembling slightly as a shadow passes by, the room silent and still.
a hand hovering over a lit match, the flame reflecting in the eyes of someone watching
a dark sky filled with lightning, rain slashing across a windowpane
a silent room with a single light on, casting long shadows across the walls
a wet floor reflecting a stormy sky, with a lone figure standing in the center
a matchstick held in a trembling hand, flame flickering in the wind
two figures standing on opposite sides of a battlefield, weapons raised
a hand clutching a heart-shaped locket, tears streaking down the face
a room swallowed by darkness, with only a faint glow from a distant window
a small, dimly lit room with a single chair and a flickering lamp
a pale moon hanging over a stormy sea, casting silver light on the waves
a silver blade resting on a table, reflecting the dim light of a single lamp
a figure kneeling in the rain, arms outstretched toward a distant light
a silent room with a faint hum in the air, the only sound the ticking of a clock

meaning.story: The song is a powerful assertion of self-worth and emotional independence, as the narrator refuses to be a crutch or a source of pain for someone who is emotionally unstable and destructive. It reflects the internal struggle of loving someone who constantly pulls the narrator back into cycles of hurt, while also expressing a quiet hope that the other person might one day find peace without needing the narrator to be their salvation.

sections.name: Intro - Voicemail, Soft Female Voice
sections.interpretation: The intro sets the tone of a relationship that has become a recurring cycle of pain and return.
sections.keyLine: but I can’t keep being the place you go when you wanna fall apart

sections.name: Chorus - Female Lead
sections.interpretation: A declaration of refusal to be the emotional fuel for someone else's turmoil.
sections.keyLine: I won’t be your fire just because you miss the burn

sections.name: Verse 1 - Female Lead, Melodic
sections.interpretation: The narrator is aware of the patterns of pain and the role she plays in them.
sections.keyLine: every time I answer I become what you can’t heal

sections.name: Pre-Chorus - Female Lead
sections.interpretation: The narrator is shifting from being a source of pain to a guardian of her own emotional boundaries.
sections.keyLine: If I love you at all I can’t be your fire

sections.name: Verse 2 - Male Ghost Vocal, Fragile Melodic Rap
sections.interpretation: The male voice reveals the internal chaos and emotional dependency that drives the relationship.
sections.keyLine: I only feel close when you’re angry at me

sections.name: Bridge - Duet, Slow Build
sections.interpretation: A moment of vulnerability and mutual understanding between the two voices.
sections.keyLine: I’m saying no because I love you enough not to become another wound

sections.name: Breakdown - Half-Time, Big Guitars
sections.interpretation: A reflection on the complexity of love, memory, and the desire for emotional freedom.
sections.keyLine: I can love you and leave the room

sections.name: Final Chorus - Full Band, Both Voices
sections.interpretation: A unified and powerful resolution to the emotional conflict and a reaffirmation of self-respect.
sections.keyLine: but I won’t be your reason to keep breaking down

keyLines: I won’t be your fire just because you miss the burn
every time I answer I become what you can’t heal
If I love you at all I can’t be your fire
I only feel close when you’re angry at me
I’m saying no because I love you enough not to become another wound
I can love you and leave the room
but I won’t be your reason to keep breaking down

styleHint: Emo-rap pop-punk response anthem, 85 BPM half-time feel with 170 BPM double-time trap hats, dark minor key, huge viral hook, late-night toxic-love voicemail energy. Start intimate: rain ambience, muted electric guitar, distant phone buzz, soft wounded female vocal. Build into distorted guitars, punchy live snare, deep sub bass, glossy trap drums, stadium-size chorus, layered “no no no” gang vocals, male ghost vocal ad-libs in the background. Vocals should feel raw, cracked, melodic, desperate, and instantly singable; verses are vulnerable melodic rap, chorus is explosive pop-punk emo singalong. Dynamic structure: quiet intro, tense verse, pre-chorus lift, massive chorus, stripped bridge, final chorus with stacked harmonies. Theme: refusing to keep hurting someone you still love. Make it cinematic, heartbreaking, rebellious, catchy, modern, and replayable. Short viral singalong chorus, memorable one-line hook: “I won’t be your fire.” No cheesy theater, no happy pop, no over-rapping.
